methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
MaverickX is a Seed-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Eric Herrera that has raised $19M to date. The valuation analysis incorporates a peer universe of 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) alongside 1 public comparable and a broader set of 8 frontier-tech energy peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $48M. Peer-median analysis across the 3-company comparable set yielded a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M (Steady Energy) to $200M (Graphyte). The peer-median method carries greater weight given direct sector and stage alignment.

Triangulation across both methods produces a valuation range of $40M to $172M, with a median of $99M. The wide spread reflects the substantial dispersion in early-stage climate-tech valuations and the limited precision of pre-revenue or early-revenue stage companies. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peers, path to near-term revenue or technology de-risking milestones, and the sensitivity of comparable valuations to underlying assumptions about market timing and investor appetite in the energy transition sector.
